content_body:
A Refreshing Mountain Getaway Close to Atlanta


Southern Living magazine hails Blue Ridge, Georgia as one of the “coolest mountain towns in North Georgia.” In fact, it ranked #3 in a 2022 reader poll of the 15 best mountain towns in the South. Just 90 minutes north of Atlanta and bordering Tennessee and North Carolina, Blue Ridge is an easy escape for a weekend getaway or a longer stay. Boasting a variety of indoor and outdoor activities for all ages, there's things to do in Blue Ridge for every type of visit. 

Our luxury cabins in Blue Ridge GA make it simple to trade the city for the mountains, whether you’re planning a family vacation, couples retreat, or girls' getaway. With hot tubs, game rooms, and views of the mountains, river, or lake, you’ll have the perfect backdrop for exploring North Georgia or simply relaxing in style.

Must-Visit Mountain Town in Georgia

Blue Ridge is located at the beautiful southern end of the Appalachian Mountains and is known as one of the best mountain spots in the Southeast. Visitors can explore boutique shops, enjoy the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway, or relax on Lake Blue Ridge. There's truly something for everyone, whether you're into outdoor adventures or prefer a leisurely afternoon through town.

Blue Ridge GA Things to Do

With so many free things to do in this part of North GA, you may not experience it all in one weekend! Many guests return season after season to check more adventures off their Blue Ridge bucket list. Here's just a taste of outdoor activities in Blue Ridge!

Hiking in Blue Ridge

Outdoor enthusiasts can explore the Chattahoochee National Forest, with access to the Appalachian Trail and Benton MacKaye Trail. Short hikes to Falls Branch Falls or Long Creek Falls reward you with cascading waterfalls and photo-worthy moments. These hidden treasures are a must on your North Georgia vacation!

After a day on the trail, your Blue Ridge GA cabin rental with hot tub is the perfect place to rest your legs.

Lake Blue Ridge Activities

Spend the day boating, paddleboarding, or fishing in crystal-clear waters surrounded by national forest. As the sun sets, head back to your lake-view luxury cabin for dinner on the deck and a quiet evening by the fire.

Toccoa River Tubing, Kayaking, or Fishing

The Toccoa River, which runs through Blue Ridge from the lake to Tennessee, offers tubing, kayaking, and excellent fly fishing. Marvel at the historic Cherokee Indian Fish Traps which have been preserved through the centuries and create ideal opportunities for a guided fly-fishing trip. Our riverfront cabins in Blue Ridge GA keep you close to the action.

Fishing in Blue Ridge, GA

Known as the Trout Capital of Georgia, Blue Ridge is a dream destination for anglers. With over 100 miles of rivers, brooks, and streams, it’s easy to hook rainbow and brown trout in the pristine waters of Fannin County. Noontootla Creek is a local favorite. Be sure to have your fishing license! 

Just ask us where to fish and we can hook you up with the best fishing guides in North Georgia. Stay in a cabin with easy water access and you can fish from your doorstep.

Wineries and Vineyards in Blue Ridge

Your visit to the North GA mountains would not be complete without visiting the area's award-winning wineries. Explore the different wineries sprinkled throughout Blue Ridge. Sit outside and enjoy the stunning mountain views while sipping local wines. Many of our luxury cabins are just a short drive from tasting rooms.

Family Activities in Blue Ridge

Looking for a fun family vacation in North Georgia? Blue Ridge has plenty of family things to do to keep kids entertained. In downtown Blue Ridge, you can find many attractions. Visit Vertigo Pinball, Huck’s General Store, and Deaf Man Vinyl.

For outdoor fun, families with small children can enjoy the Swinging Bridge. They can hike to Falls Branch Falls, which is easy for little legs. Boating with Blue Ridge Boat Rentals on Lake Blue Ridge is also a fun option. Additionally, they can try gem mining at Lilly Pad Village or Aska Mining Company.

Explore Downtown Blue Ridge, GA

Downtown Blue Ridge boasts a blend of unique boutiques, top-notch dining, and charming breweries and wineries. Hop on the scenic train ride through the North Georgia mountains, and soak in the beauty of the Toccoa River as it winds through the countryside. When you're day of exploration comes to a close, our cabins close to downtown Blue Ridge are just minutes away. 

Blue Ridge, GA Weather by Season

Blue Ridge enjoys mostly temperate weather year-round. Experiencing all four seasons in its full array of beauty is a huge draw for many travelers. Browse Blue Ridge's seasonal things to do here! 

Spring in Blue Ridge

Springtime in Blue Ridge is a feast for the eyes. By April, the landscape is alive with vibrant green hues, making it an ideal time to explore the mountains or relax by the outdoor fireplace in the cool evenings. 

Summer in Blue Ridge

Days in the 80s and cool evenings make summer ideal for boating, hiking, and tubing. Grill on your deck or soak in the hot tub after an active day.

Fall in Blue Ridge

Autumn in Blue Ridge is nothing short of magical. October brings spectacular foliage, mild days, and crisp evenings. With daytime temperatures in the 70s and crisp evenings in the 50s, it's the perfect time for leaf-peeping and cozying up in a luxury cabin.

 It’s a favorite time for guests to book romantic Blue Ridge GA getaways or family trips. 

Winters in Blue Ridge

Wintertime in Blue Ridge can be magical, beginning with the Christmas spirit displayed throughout the town. Light-up Blue Ridge is a popular historic festival after Thanksgiving, where the entire town is beautifully lit, along with the massive Christmas Tree in the park. Temperatures are generally comfortably cool, with daytimes in the 50s and nights in the 30s to 40s.

Though January and February are the slower months, there is a certain mystique during this time as well - mountains will sparkle in the morning sun after Jack Frost has visited, leaving its frosty sheen. Temperatures are much cooler during these months, with highs in the 40s and lows dipping in the 20s. There will be many days sprinkled during these months that are more temperate, perfect for enjoying outdoor activities with no crowds.

Even on cold days, our cabins’ fireplaces, plush furnishings, and panoramic views make staying in a treat.

When Does It Snow in Blue Ridge?

If you’re looking for snow in Blue Ridge, you’re most likely to enjoy snowy days from January to early March. On occasion, Blue Ridge has enjoyed a white Christmas, but only a handful of times in the last 20 years.

Blue Ridge Cabin Rentals: The Ultimate Relaxation


Blue Ridge Lake Sanctuary

The best part of Blue Ridge might just be your luxurious Georgia mountain cabin retreat. Many visitors find that once they arrive at their vacation rental, they never want to leave! Our luxury cabins in BLue Ridge provide a great mix of comfort and adventure. 

From large group luxury cabins to pet-friendly cabin rentals in Blue Ridge, our luxury cabins offer fantastic amenities from hot tubs and fire pits to game rooms and King/Queen Suites. Most people spend a lot of time on the decks overlooking mountain vistas or the rushing waters of the Toccoa River.

Why Blue Ridge, GA Is the Perfect Escape


Riverview Lodge

If you’re looking for a destination where you can slow down, connect with nature, and rejuvenate your soul, Blue Ridge, GA, is the place to be. From the tranquility of the Appalachian Mountains to the warm hospitality of its people, Blue Ridge offers a memorable escape that will have you coming back year after year. 

Call us today and start planning your North GA Blue Ridge getaway ~ 706-432-2140. Browse our full inventory of luxury cabins to find your perfect match.
title: About Blue Ridge, GA
